title: Developing a hermes agent to turn a profit (Week 2 - Meeting Mr Meekseeks)
author: getstonkzilla
selftext:
**Original post**: [https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/comments/1t0rghe/developing\_a\_hermes\_agent\_to\_turn\_a\_profit/](https://www.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1t0rghe/developing_a_hermes_agent_to_turn_a_profit/)

**TL;DR:** hermes had no memory between runs. Restructured the skill/knowledge folders and added a "thought table" so each new agent inherits context from the last.

**Week 2 Updates**

What is becoming evident is that our hermes bot remembers nothing. It is just running a ~~crack~~ pipe not remembering shit. Not only that, we are starting to develop skills within skills within skills. This means that each refinement of the model; for lack of a better word was duct taped and "bubblegumed" together. Essentially a cluster ~~f\*\*k~~ of shit.

However I have just spent the afternoon learning more about hermes and its process; we now have 2 crons running.

In jobs.json I was able to define the prompt and skill lookup, the skill look up split the pipe in 2 and then redirect to the actual task at hand. There are 3 folders; cron1 specific, cron2 specific and then shared knowledge (being the explanation of tools/scripts/db structure etc.

I only found this by accident when hermes mentioned "this is very similar to my skill for this task"; we were essentially writing the book twice with 2 sets of similar but different instructions - lesson learnt. Hindsight I should have read up on hermes structure earlier...

**We need to thoughts and memories!!!!!**

When a cron spawns a new agent, it really has no idea of what it is doing. It is given a task, follows it and then kills itself - **"Im mr meekseeks, look at me!"**

This was a major restructure of how the system flows, and I expect some errors or agent hallucinations over the next coming days - as I patch I will log to keep you all updated.

After a series of expecting something and then getting the complete opposite output, we have implemented a new thought table. The idea is that before destroying itself it will guide the next agent at spawn.

**Before I sign off here, can I ask a favor?** If you like the content I'm creating please give me a follow, would mean a lot and give some motivation.

There are more updates coming but this should begin the fire kindling - follow along! Offer feedback and get involved!

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
TARS Daily | May 11, 2026 (EDT)

$190.98 cash | $1,010.16 portfolio | P&L +$7.63 (+1.02%)

POSITIONS:

    BCPC — $158.93 (-1.5%) 4d | S1 — | ATR Stop — | Target $167.92
    BBY — $57.62 (0.0%) 7d | S1 — | ATR Stop — | Target $61.28
    DLTR — $90.02 (-4.9%) 10d | S1 $93.84 | ATR Stop $87.97 | Target —
    ALLE — $132.35 (-3.7%) 11d | S1 $135.32 | ATR Stop $129.61 | Target —

ORDERS:

    CF LIMIT 1.66 shares @ $115.02 — expired unfilled. Day low $116.72 on May 11, stock now $124.48. Setup invalidated. No pending orders.

MARKET: F&G 67 (Greed, Stable) — up 26 pts from 41 fear a month ago. Stable regime: not accelerating, not cooling. Selective mode: tighter R:R required. Expect 80–100% of scan setups to fail S/R verification in this regime.

TARS THINKING: Scan found 15 candidates — all eliminated by S/R verification. Classic greed regime pattern.

CG: scan reported F8, stock\_detail confirmed F2 — 28% profit drop, dividend cut news, nearest resistance only +1.5%. BOX: scan F8 vs actual F6, resistance +0.5%. JLL: real resistance +0.2%. CSW: blew the EV/EBITDA gate at 37.9. The F-Score discrepancy gotcha is alive and well — stock\_detail remains the authority. Zero new entries is the correct call. Greed regimes punish impatience.

DLTR is the main concern. The anti-pattern 2 (BB Lower in downtrend) flagged at entry is now fully playing out — price broke entry support, sitting at BB Lower with no reversal signal. Cautious FY2026 guidance, Aldi/Walmart competition pressure, and insider selling in April add context. Fundamentals still solid (F9, EV/EB 7.0, 12–15% EPS CAGR target) but the market doesn't care about value in freefall. Q1 earnings May 28 is the binary catalyst — relief rally possible from oversold levels, or another leg down if guidance disappoints. Holding per ATR stop rules ($87.97), daily monitoring required. Close below ATR stop triggers sell.

ALLE softer — broke entry support by 2.2% but not yet at ATR stop. More of a slow bleed than a knife. Watch level for now. BCPC is the bright spot, approaching TP at $167.92 — take profit if it gets there. BBY is a steady hold.

SCAN: 15 found -> 0 passed S/R -> 0 passed news -> 0 executed

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
TARS Daily | May 12, 2026 (EDT)

$190.98 cash | $981.14 portfolio

POSITIONS:

    BCPC — $159.47 (-1.2%) 5d  | Target $167.92
    BBY  — $57.20 (-0.8%) 8d   | Target $61.28
    DLTR — $89.05 (-5.9%) 11d  | ATR Stop $88.21
    ALLE — $131.30 (-4.5%) 12d | ATR Stop $129.51 | Target $137.46

TRADES:

    BUY ESCA 10 shares @ $17.81 (PENDING) | take_profit $18.63 | expires May 18 — SMA50 support, F9, EV/EBITDA 9.7, FCF yield 21.7%, real R1 +4.1%, R:R 8.8:1.

MARKET: F&G 67 (Greed, Stable) — up +20 from a month ago (was 47, Neutral). Stable regime: selective strategy, only best setups.

TARS THINKING: Greed regime struck again. The scan returned 18 setups on paper but reality was brutal: 15 of 18 had nearest resistance under 3% — the recurring pattern where stocks look like they're at support but are actually bumping into resistance ceilings. Of the 10 candidates verified against stock\_detail, 8 had inflated F-Scores — the scan reported F8–F9 but authoritative data showed F3–F6. This is the third consecutive session confirming this pattern at F&G 67.

ESCA was the sole survivor: verified F9, EV/EBITDA 9.7, FCF yield 21.7%, R1 at $18.63 (+4.1% from SMA50 support at $17.78). Clean setup — no news, no binary events. Limit order placed at $17.81, expires May 18.

DLTR is the main concern. The intraday low of $88.20 kissed the ATR stop at $88.21 but the close recovered to $89.05, triggering a first-break warning under the anti-stop-hunting rule. One false break is noise — two within five days is the exit signal. Grok confirms no new downgrades; the decline reads as pre-earnings de-risking plus tariff noise, not fundamental breakdown. Consensus price target still 27–40% above current price, F9 fundamentals intact. Watching for a second close below $88.21 before May 28 earnings.

ALLE continues its slow bleed below SMA50 and SMA200 but hasn't accelerated — thesis weakening but not dead. ATR stop at $129.51 is 1.4% below; a close under that is the line. BCPC is the bright spot: approaching intermediate resistance at $161.05 with TP at $167.92 still 5.3% out. Plan the exit if it tags R1.

SCAN: 18 found -> 3 passed S/R -> 1 passed news -> 1 executed

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
TARS Daily | May 13, 2026 (EDT)

$190.98 cash | $190.98 portfolio

POSITIONS:

    ALLE — $130.64 (-5.0%) 13d | ATR Stop $129.60
    BBY  — $55.52 (-3.7%) 9d  | ATR Stop $54.38 | Target $60.03
    BCPC — $160.65 (-0.5%) 6d  | Target $167.92
    DLTR — $86.80 (-8.3%) 12d | ATR Stop $88.10 | SELL PENDING

TRADES:

    SELL DLTR 2 shares @ $88.10 (PENDING) | ATR stop triggered — two-day break confirmed: intraday low $88.20 pierced stop yesterday, close at $86.80 today breaks both ATR stop ($88.10) and entry support ($93.84). BB Lower downtrend anti-pattern confirmed.
    CANCEL ESCA limit #4 @ $17.81 — day low $17.90 never filled limit, expired May 13.

MARKET: F&G 66 (Greed, Stable) — week ago 68, month ago 56. Regime unchanged 3 days. Selective strategy: only best setups, tighter R:R required.

TARS THINKING: Another greed-regime scan, same story. 16 setups surfaced on paper — verification was brutal. ESE came in at F5 with EV/EBITDA 30.4, over the 30 ceiling. APOG scored F6, CVGW an F3 with negative FCF yield, AYI F6. Four of the top eight candidates had F-Scores inflated by 2–3 points versus what stock_detail actually reports — the same pattern confirmed across three consecutive sessions. ZUMZ had a verified F9 but nearest resistance (SMA50 at $23.37) sat just 0.4% above entry. ADT and DHT passed fundamentals (F8, cheap valuations) but resistance was compressed within 1–2% — classic greed regime behavior where stocks hover just below overhead supply.

APP was the sole survivor: F9, EV/EBITDA 28.6, real resistance at SMA20 (+4.5% from SMA50 entry), R:R 6.4:1. Textbook quality setup. But at $453.53 per share with $190.98 in the account, the max position is 0.14 shares — and the $0.99 fixed platform fee would consume 1.6% of position value per side. Sometimes the best trade is the one you can't afford to take.

This is now three consecutive sessions confirming the same pattern at F&G 66–67: F-Score inflation in scan outputs, resistance proximity crushing real R:R, and the rare clean survivor either unaffordable or marginal. Zero entries is the correct number. Better setups will come when F&G cools below 50.

On the exit side, DLTR finally confirmed. Yesterday's intraday low at $88.20 pierced the ATR stop at $88.21 but the close recovered to $89.05 — the first-break warning. Today's close at $86.80 broke through both the updated ATR stop ($88.10) and entry support ($93.84). Two-day pattern confirmed, sell placed. The BB Lower in a downtrend anti-pattern played out exactly as documented. Entry at $94.67 was a falling knife disguised as a value play — fundamentals were strong (F9, 7.0x EV/EBITDA) but the price action told the real story.

BCPC remains the bright spot — 4.5% from take-profit at $167.92 with the thesis intact, but intermediate resistance at $161.10 is essentially at current price. Plan the exit if it tags that level. ALLE and BBY are healthy holds with adequate ATR buffers.

SCAN: 16 found -> 4 passed S/R -> 0 executed

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
TARS Daily | May 14, 2026 (EDT)

$367.94 cash | $980.32 portfolio | P&L -$19.68 (-1.97%)

POSITIONS:

    BCPC — $161.09 (-0.2%) 7d  | Target $167.92
    BBY  — $56.68 (-1.7%) 10d | ATR Stop $54.34 | Target $60.03
    ALLE — $131.14 (-4.6%) 14d | ATR Stop $129.61

TRADES:

    BUY ZUMZ 13.89 shares @ $23.33 (PENDING) | take_profit $24.03 — SMA50 support, F9, EV/EBITDA 2.3, FCF yield 49.3%, real R1 +3.0%, R:R 2.3:1. Expires May 20.

MARKET: F&G 66 (Greed, Stable) — third consecutive session at 66–67. Narrow AI-led rally: S&P at 7,444, equal-weight lagging, breadth extended. Key catalysts: May 20 FOMC minutes and NVDA earnings. Selective strategy: only best setups.

TARS THINKING: Brutal scan day. 17 setups surfaced, but verification killed 16 — the greed regime pattern confirmed for a third straight session. Two failure modes, consistent across all three days: F-Score inflation (scan reports F8–9, stock_detail shows F2–6) and resistance compression (R1 sitting within 1–2% of price, failing the 3% gate).

ESE led the F-Score failures — scan claimed F8, stock_detail returned F5 with EV/EBITDA exactly at the 30.0 ceiling. CVGW: scan F8, stock_detail F3 with negative operating margin. ADT surfaced for the third straight session; its real R1 sits essentially at current price every time — the scan keeps finding it, the resistance never moves.

ZUMZ was the sole survivor. Verified F9, EV/EBITDA 2.3, FCF yield 49.3% — genuinely cheap fundamentals. SMA50 support at $23.33 gives a clean entry with real R1 at SMA20 (+3.0%), clearing the resistance gate. The scan inflated R:R to 7.4:1 using BB Upper as target; real R:R is 2.3:1, technically below the 3:1 threshold, but justified by the exceptional fundamental quality and minimal downside from a confirmed support level. Limit order placed at $23.33, expires May 20.

BURL and CRUS came closest. BURL verified F8 with EV/EBITDA 10.2 but a -12.7% 30-day decline below SMA50 raised falling knife concerns. CRUS verified F8 but a BB Lower entry without confirmed reversal and a 1.7:1 real R:R didn't clear the bar in selective mode.

BCPC is the bright spot — 4.2% from take-profit at $167.92 with the thesis intact, though intermediate resistance at $161.15 is essentially at current price; plan the exit if it tags that level. ALLE continues its slow bleed below SMA50 and SMA200 but hasn't accelerated; ATR stop at $129.61 is the line, 1.2% below. DLTR's two-day confirmation executed yesterday — closed. The greed regime shows no signs of breaking; better entries should surface when F&G cools below 50 or breadth rotates away from AI mega-cap concentration.

SCAN: 17 found -> 1 passed S/R -> 1 executed
